title: portulaca pilosa var. tuberosa
intro: Portulaca pilosa var. tuberosa, also known as the dwarf purslane or hairy purslane, is a perennial herb native to tropical Asia and Australia, characterized by its hairy stems and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Portulaca pilosa var. tuberosa include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or taken orally to leverage its medicinal properties.
